lines.en_lit: “And the cloud covered him,” the mountain, “And He called to Moses”; Moses and all of the Jewish people were standing The verse comes only to accord deference to Moses, Rabbi Natan says: rather, the verse comes only to cleanse the food and drink that was in his intestines, to render him like the ministering angels

Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash says: The verse comes only to intimidate so that the Torah would be delivered with reverence, with quaking and with trembling, as it is stated: “Serve the Lord with awe, and rejoice with trembling” What “and rejoice with trembling”? Rav Adda bar Mattana said Rav said: Where there is joy of there will be trembling there.

what do Rabbi Yosei HaGelili and Rabbi Akiva disagree? to the dispute between these tanna’im, as it was taught On the sixth of the month the Torah, was given to the Jewish people. Rabbi Yosei says: on the seventh of one who said on the sixth, was given on the sixth, and on the seventh ascended as it is written: “And He called to Moses on the seventh day” one who said on the seventh it was given on the seventh and Moses ascended on the seventh, as it is written: “And he called to Moses on the seventh day.”

Rabbi Yosei HaGelili holds in accordance with the first tanna who said on the sixth that the Torah was given; therefore, this incident occurred after the Ten Commandments. “And the glory of the Lord abode on Mount Sinai and the cloud covered him for six days” Moses, and He called to Moses on the seventh day to receive the rest of the Torah. As, should it enter your mind “And the glory of the Lord abode” from the New Moon “And the cloud covered it,” the mountain; “And He called to Moses on the seventh day,” didn’t they receive on the sixth and the cloud already departed on the sixth

And Rabbi Akiva holds in accordance with Rabbi Yosei, who said that on the seventh of the month Torah was given to the Jewish people. Granted, Rabbi Akiva that that you find: On the seventeenth of Tammuz the tablets were shattered, twenty-four of Sivan and sixteen of Tammuz; they total the forty days that he was on the mountain. On the seventeenth of Tammuz he descended and came and shattered tablets.

However, according to Rabbi Yosei HaGelili, who said: six of sequestering forty that mountain, the tablets were not shattered until the twenty-third of Tammuz, Rabbi Yosei HaGelili said to you: The forty that mountain include the six of sequestering.

The Master said “And He called to Moses,” Moses and all of the Jewish people were standing supports Rabbi Elazar, as Rabbi Elazar said “And He called to Moses,” Moses and all of the Jewish people were standing and the verse comes only to accord deference to Moses.

raises an objection: voice to voice unto Moses heard and all of the Jewish people did not hear not difficult. This at Sinai. That at Tent of Meeting. Or if you wish, say not difficult; calling speaking,

Rabbi Zerika raised a contradiction between verses before Rabbi Elazar, and some say Rabbi Zerika said Rabbi Elazar raised a contradiction: It is written “And Moses was not able to enter into the Tent of Meeting because the cloud dwelt on it” And it is written “And Moses came into the cloud” This teaches that the Holy One, Blessed be He, grabbed Moses and brought him into the cloud

The school of Rabbi Yishmael taught: It is stated here: into and it is stated below, “And the children of Israel went into the sea Just as below, a path as it is written: “And the water was a wall for them” here too, a path

“And He called unto Moses, and spoke Why calling before speaking, The Torah is teaching etiquette: A person not say anything to another unless he calls him supports Rabbi Ḥanina, as Rabbi Ḥanina said: A person should not say anything to another unless he calls him “Saying,” Rabbi Musya, grandson of Rabbi Masya, said in the name of Rabbi Musya the Great: From where to one who tells another matter, that not say it until says to him: Go and tell others? As it is stated: “And the Lord spoke to him from the Tent of Meeting, saying [lemor].”

by inference both maintain inauguration, all that are written in its invalidates As it is stated: Rabbi Yoḥanan and Rabbi Ḥanina One said: all that are written in its invalidates And one said: A matter that invalidates throughout the generations invalidates a matter that does not invalidate throughout the generations does not invalidate

Conclude Rabbi Yoḥanan is the one who said: all that are written in its invalidates from that Rabbi Shimon ben Lakish says to Rabbi Yoḥanan: Just as inauguration, all that are written in its invalidates and did not respond not anything, Conclude
